verses.text: At the pool of Bethesda the sick wait for the moving of the water. Helpless waiting. Many lie longing for a cure beyond their reach.
At the pool of Bethesda the sick wait for the moving of the water. Helpless waiting. Many lie longing for a cure beyond their reach.
At the pool of Bethesda the sick wait for the moving of the water. Helpless waiting. Many lie longing for a cure beyond their reach.
At the pool of Bethesda the sick wait for the moving of the water. Helpless waiting. Many lie longing for a cure beyond their reach.
A man sick thirty-eight years; 'Wilt thou be made whole? ... Rise, take up thy bed, and walk'; he is healed at once. Sovereign cure. Christ heals where no help could come.
A man sick thirty-eight years; 'Wilt thou be made whole? ... Rise, take up thy bed, and walk'; he is healed at once. Sovereign cure. Christ heals where no help could come.
A man sick thirty-eight years; 'Wilt thou be made whole? ... Rise, take up thy bed, and walk'; he is healed at once. Sovereign cure. Christ heals where no help could come.
A man sick thirty-eight years; 'Wilt thou be made whole? ... Rise, take up thy bed, and walk'; he is healed at once. Sovereign cure. Christ heals where no help could come.
A man sick thirty-eight years; 'Wilt thou be made whole? ... Rise, take up thy bed, and walk'; he is healed at once. Sovereign cure. Christ heals where no help could come.
It is the sabbath; they fault him for carrying his bed; he knew not who healed him. Petty zeal. They guard the day and ignore the mercy.
It is the sabbath; they fault him for carrying his bed; he knew not who healed him. Petty zeal. They guard the day and ignore the mercy.
It is the sabbath; they fault him for carrying his bed; he knew not who healed him. Petty zeal. They guard the day and ignore the mercy.
It is the sabbath; they fault him for carrying his bed; he knew not who healed him. Petty zeal. They guard the day and ignore the mercy.
Jesus finds him, 'sin no more'; he tells the Jews, who persecute Jesus for sabbath healing. Mercy resented. Doing good provokes the hard of heart.
Jesus finds him, 'sin no more'; he tells the Jews, who persecute Jesus for sabbath healing. Mercy resented. Doing good provokes the hard of heart.
Jesus finds him, 'sin no more'; he tells the Jews, who persecute Jesus for sabbath healing. Mercy resented. Doing good provokes the hard of heart.
'My Father worketh hitherto, and I work'; they seek to kill Him for 'making himself equal with God.' Divine claim. He calls God His own Father in a unique sense.
'My Father worketh hitherto, and I work'; they seek to kill Him for 'making himself equal with God.' Divine claim. He calls God His own Father in a unique sense.
The Son does only what He sees the Father do; 'the Son quickeneth whom he will.' Perfect union. The Son works in step with the Father, and gives life.
The Son does only what He sees the Father do; 'the Son quickeneth whom he will.' Perfect union. The Son works in step with the Father, and gives life.
The Son does only what He sees the Father do; 'the Son quickeneth whom he will.' Perfect union. The Son works in step with the Father, and gives life.
The Father has given all judgment to the Son; 'he that... believeth... hath everlasting life.' Life now. Faith in Christ already passes from death to life.
The Father has given all judgment to the Son; 'he that... believeth... hath everlasting life.' Life now. Faith in Christ already passes from death to life.
The Father has given all judgment to the Son; 'he that... believeth... hath everlasting life.' Life now. Faith in Christ already passes from death to life.
The dead shall hear the Son's voice and live; He has authority to judge. Voice of life. The same Lord gives life and will judge all.
The dead shall hear the Son's voice and live; He has authority to judge. Voice of life. The same Lord gives life and will judge all.
The dead shall hear the Son's voice and live; He has authority to judge. Voice of life. The same Lord gives life and will judge all.
All in the graves shall hear His voice, to 'the resurrection of life' or 'of damnation.' Two risings. The final resurrection divides for ever.
All in the graves shall hear His voice, to 'the resurrection of life' or 'of damnation.' Two risings. The final resurrection divides for ever.
'I seek not mine own will, but the will of the Father'; another bears witness of Me. Selfless obedience. He acts wholly for the Father's glory.
'I seek not mine own will, but the will of the Father'; another bears witness of Me. Selfless obedience. He acts wholly for the Father's glory.
'I seek not mine own will, but the will of the Father'; another bears witness of Me. Selfless obedience. He acts wholly for the Father's glory.
John bore witness; he was 'a burning and a shining light.' A true lamp. The forerunner shone to point men to Christ.
John bore witness; he was 'a burning and a shining light.' A true lamp. The forerunner shone to point men to Christ.
John bore witness; he was 'a burning and a shining light.' A true lamp. The forerunner shone to point men to Christ.
His works and the Father's testimony witness to Him; yet they have not God's word abiding. Witness refused. They will not receive the proof set before them.
His works and the Father's testimony witness to Him; yet they have not God's word abiding. Witness refused. They will not receive the proof set before them.
His works and the Father's testimony witness to Him; yet they have not God's word abiding. Witness refused. They will not receive the proof set before them.
'Search the scriptures'; they testify of Me; 'ye will not come to me, that ye might have life.' Missed Christ. They read the word yet reject the One it speaks of.
'Search the scriptures'; they testify of Me; 'ye will not come to me, that ye might have life.' Missed Christ. They read the word yet reject the One it speaks of.
I receive not honour from men; how can ye believe, who seek honour 'one of another'? Pride blinds. The love of men's praise shuts out faith.
I receive not honour from men; how can ye believe, who seek honour 'one of another'? Pride blinds. The love of men's praise shuts out faith.
I receive not honour from men; how can ye believe, who seek honour 'one of another'? Pride blinds. The love of men's praise shuts out faith.
I receive not honour from men; how can ye believe, who seek honour 'one of another'? Pride blinds. The love of men's praise shuts out faith.
Moses, in whom ye trust, accuses you; if ye believe not his writings, how My words? Moses points to Christ. To reject Christ is to misread Moses.
